title: $2.2M portfolio — consistency mattered more than anything else
author: Swimming-Salary9550
selftext:
I don’t post often, but this felt like a milestone worth sharing.

Just crossed \~$2.2M CAD across my accounts, with about +44% over the past year. It’s been a strong run, but it definitely didn’t happen overnight.

There were plenty of mistakes along the way — chasing hype, overtrading, and underestimating risk early on. What made the biggest difference for me over time was sticking to a plan, staying consistent, and learning to manage downside better.

The past year has been great on paper, but I try not to get too comfortable. Markets change fast, and I’ve learned that protecting capital matters just as much as growing it.

Still a lot to learn, still a long way to go — but grateful for the progress so far.
